key_themes: Analyst Moderate Buy consensus with ~$31 price targets
summary: AT&T (T) sentiment is solidly positive in April 2026, with the stock up ~12% YTD near $26.50 on strong fiber growth momentum, the closed Lumen fiber deal adding millions of passings/subscribers, raised free cash flow guidance above $18B for the year, and plans to return $45 billion to shareholders.[[1]](https://www.tikr.com/blog/att-is-up-12-year-to-date-in-2026-heres-where-the-stock-could-go-next)[[2]](https://www.thestreet.com/investing/stocks/how-much-to-invest-in-att-stock-for-1000-in-2026-annual-dividends) Analysts maintain a Moderate Buy/Overweight stance with an average 12-month target of ~$30.74 (15-16% upside), highest targets to $36. Q1 2026 earnings (due April 22) are expected to show EPS ~$0.55; new segment reporting and the fiber narrative dominate investor focus despite legacy business and ARPU headwinds.[[3]](https://www.marketbeat.com/stocks/NYSE/T/forecast/)[[4]](https://finance.yahoo.com/markets/stocks/articles/insights-t-t-q1-wall-131508018.html)
sources: 20+ news/analyst articles (TIKR, Yahoo Finance, MarketBeat, Motley Fool), 25+ X posts (semantic + keyword searches since Mar 2026)
